alignments.preprint_claim_text: The reinforcement learning framework for retail robo-advising learns the investor's risk preference over time by observing her portfolio choices.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework learns the investor’s risk preference over time by observing her portfolio choices in different market environments.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same core claim but adds the contextual phrase "in different market environments," which clarifies the setting without changing the claim's substance or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The exploration-exploitation algorithm effectively balances costly solicitations of portfolio choices with autonomous trading decisions.
alignments.rationale: No publication claim corresponds to the specific exploration-exploitation solicitation vs autonomous trading tradeoff, so this preprint claim appears omitted in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithm's value function converges to the optimal value function of an omniscient robo-advisor in a number of periods that is polynomial in the state and action space.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approximate value function constructed by the algorithm converges to the value function of an omniscient robo-advisor over a number of periods that is polynomial in the state and action space.
alignments.rationale: Both statements assert polynomial-time convergence of the algorithm's value function to that of an omniscient robo-advisor; wording is essentially equivalent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The robo-advisor may outperform a stand-alone investor by correcting for the investor's mistakes, regardless of the investor's opportunity cost for making portfolio decisions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The robo-advisor may outperform a stand-alone investor by correcting for the investor’s mistakes.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the core claim that the robo-advisor can outperform by correcting mistakes but drops the preprint's additional unconditional qualifier about opportunity cost, narrowing the scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper presents an unsupervised deep learning-based numerical approach for solving partial differential equations (PDEs).
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper presents an unsupervised deep learning-based numerical approach for solving partial differential equations (PDEs).
alignments.rationale: The contribution statement is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach utilizes deep neural networks to approximate solutions of PDEs through compositional construction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach utilizes deep neural networks to approximate solutions of PDEs through compositional construction.
alignments.rationale: The description of the method is carried over unchanged into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study focuses on the first-order system least-squares (FOSLS) functional for second-order elliptic PDEs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study focuses on the first-order system least-squares (FOSLS) functional for second-order elliptic PDEs.
alignments.rationale: The focus on FOSLS is preserved, but the publication marks this claim with lower certainty (medium vs high), indicating a tempered confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Numerical results for second-order elliptic PDEs in one dimension are presented.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Numerical results for second-order elliptic PDEs in one dimension are presented in the paper.
alignments.rationale: The numerical results claim is the same in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed hierarchical mixture of generators model generates samples of high quality and diversity in terms of popular GAN evaluation metrics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed hierarchical mixture of generators model generates samples of high quality and diversity in terms of popular GAN evaluation metrics.
alignments.rationale: The generation quality/diversity claim is restated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The hierarchical mixture of generators model learns a tree structure that implements hierarchical clustering with soft splits in the decision nodes.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly restate the claim about learning a tree implementing hierarchical clustering with soft splits, so this specific structural claim appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The combination of generators in the proposed model is soft, allowing the whole model to be continuous and trainable using gradient-based optimization.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The hierarchical mixture of generators model is continuous and can be trained using gradient-based optimization.
alignments.rationale: Both statements assert the model's continuity and trainability by gradient methods; the publication restates this contribution directly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The learned hierarchical structure from the model leads to knowledge extraction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The learned hierarchical structure leads to knowledge extraction.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the claim that the learned hierarchical structure enables knowledge extraction with essentially the same meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model can operate as a first step in an automatic pipeline for the estimation of nutrition content by supporting hints related to ingredient and instruction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model can operate as a first step in an automatic pipeline for the estimation of nutrition content by supporting hints related to ingredient and instruction.
alignments.rationale: The contribution regarding nutrition-estimation pipeline support is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Attention-based recipe text encoding contributes to solving the issue of high intra-class/low inter-class variability by focusing on preparation steps specific to the meal.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Attention-based recipe text encoding contributes to solving the issue of high intra-class/low inter-class variability by focusing on preparation steps specific to the meal.
alignments.rationale: The role of attention-based encoding in addressing variability is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The experimental results demonstrate the potential of such a system for recipe retrieval from images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The experimental results demonstrate the potential of such a system for recipe retrieval from images.
alignments.rationale: Experimental potential for recipe retrieval is stated the same way in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model reduces training time by making the upstream instruction sentence embedding process redundant.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Through self-attention, our model can directly process raw recipe text, making the upstream instruction sentence embedding process redundant and thus reducing training time.
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies the mechanism (self-attention) by which the model removes the need for sentence embeddings and states the reduction in training time with higher certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed deep learning framework for segmentation of arbitrary medical image volumes requires no task-specific information and no human interaction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed deep learning framework for segmentation of arbitrary medical image volumes requires no task-specific information and no human interaction.
alignments.rationale: The main contribution claim is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The system performed better than or similar to highly specialized deep learning methods across 3 separate segmentation tasks without task-specific modifications.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The system performed better than or similar to highly specialized deep learning methods across 3 separate segmentation tasks without task-specific modifications.
alignments.rationale: Performance comparison across tasks is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The system ranked 5-th and 6-th in the first and second round of the 2018 Medical Segmentation Decathlon comprising another 10 tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The system ranked 5-th and 6-th in the first and second round of the 2018 Medical Segmentation Decathlon comprising another 10 tasks.
alignments.rationale: The ranking results are restated identically in the published paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The multi-planar training approach allows the 2D model to learn a representation of the 3D image volume that fosters generalization.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The multi-planar training approach allows the 2D model to learn a representation of the 3D image volume that fosters generalization.
alignments.rationale: The explanatory claim about multi-planar training and 3D representation learning is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing GCN-based models have difficulty in well capturing complicated non-linearity of graph data. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Current GCN-based models have difficulty in well capturing complicated non-linearity of graph data. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same limitation with no substantive change in wording or scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We theoretically prove that coefficients of the neighborhood interacting terms are relatively small in current models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The coefficients of the neighborhood interacting terms are relatively small in current models, explaining why GCNs barely outperform linear models. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the theoretical claim but adds an explicit explanatory consequence (why GCNs barely outperform linear models), which clarifies and slightly broadens the implication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed GraphAIR framework models the neighborhood interaction in addition to neighborhood aggregation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed GraphAIR framework models neighborhood interaction in addition to neighborhood aggregation to better capture complicated non-linearity of graph data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same contribution but explicitly links it to better capturing non-linearity, clarifying the intended benefit without changing scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Comprehensive experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method on benchmark tasks including node classification and link prediction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Comprehensive experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method on benchmark tasks including node classification and link prediction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Experimental claim and wording are effectively identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Gym-Ignition is a new framework that creates reproducible robotic environments for reinforcement learning research. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Gym-Ignition provides a modular architecture that simplifies the interconnection with external software by allowing the simulator to be used as a C++ library. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication gives a more specific description (modular architecture / C++ library use) that supports the preprint's general claim about enabling reproducible environments.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework provides three main improvements for reinforcement learning applications: a modular architecture, support for multiple physics and rendering engines, and distributed simulation capability. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework supports multiple physics and rendering engines as plugins, simplifying their selection during execution. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves one of the three listed improvements explicitly (support for multiple engines); the preprint listed multiple items while the paper splits them across multiple claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Gym-Ignition allows developers to create robotic environments that are compatible with third-party frameworks containing reinforcement learning algorithms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Gym-Ignition provides a modular architecture that simplifies the interconnection with external software by allowing the simulator to be used as a C++ library. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication's emphasis on interconnection and library usage directly supports the preprint claim about compatibility with third-party frameworks, clarifying the mechanism.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The software architecture of Gym-Ignition abstracts the Robot and the Task, enabling execution in real-time on actual robotic platforms. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Gym-Ignition software architecture abstracts the Robot and the Task, making environments agnostic to the specific runtime. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the abstraction claim but removes the specific assertion about enabling real-time execution on actual platforms, reframing it as runtime-agnostic — a revision that narrows the concrete execution claim and tempers the confidence in that specific capability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed end-to-end framework effectively detects adversarial examples during classification without influencing the target model's performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed end-to-end framework effectively detects adversarial examples without influencing the target model's performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result claim with no substantive change.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Dense layers of DNNs carry security-sensitive information that can be analyzed to detect adversarial attacks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The prototype implementation successfully detects adversarial examples across image, natural language, and audio processing. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed weighted distributed differentially private (WD-DP) empirical risk minimization (ERM) method advances the state-of-the-art differentially private ERM methods in a distributed setting.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We guarantee differential privacy by gradient perturbation and adding Gaussian noise, advancing the state-of-the-art on gradient perturbation methods in distributed settings.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed weighted distributed differentially private (WD-DP) empirical risk minimization (ERM) method advances the state-of-the-art differentially private ERM methods in a distributed setting.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the advancement claim but does not explicitly restate the specific mechanism (gradient perturbation/Gaussian noise), so the contribution is presented in a slightly more general form.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In a distributed setting, the noise bound and the excess empirical risk bound can be improved by considering different weights held by multiple parties.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The theoretical analysis shows that the noise bound and the excess empirical risk bound can be improved by considering different weights held by multiple parties in a distributed setting.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the theoretical result about improved noise and excess risk bounds with different party weights, with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We generalize our method to non-convex loss functions that satisfy the Polyak-Lojasiewicz condition.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method generalizes to loss functions that satisfy the Polyak-Łojasiewicz condition, rather than being restricted to strongly convex loss functions.
alignments.rationale: Both versions state the same generalization to PL-condition losses; the publication explicitly contrasts with strong convexity but preserves the preprint's claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method is more reliable and improves the performance of distributed differential privacy ERM, especially when the data scale on different clients is uneven.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments demonstrate that the proposed method is more reliable and improves the performance of distributed differentially private ERM, particularly when data scales on different clients are uneven.
alignments.rationale: The experimental performance claim is maintained in the publication with equivalent wording and emphasis.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The distributed method achieves almost the same theoretical and experimental results as previous centralized methods.
alignments.rationale: This claim appears in the publication but is not present in the preprint; it adds a comparison-to-centralized-methods conclusion not previously asserted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Local Attentive Conditional Generative Adversarial Network (LAC-GAN) effectively synthesizes facial expressions based on Action Unit (AU) annotations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Local Attentive Conditional Generative Adversarial Network (LAC-GAN) effectively synthesizes facial expressions based on Action Unit (AU) annotations.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the core result that LAC-GAN effectively synthesizes AU-conditioned expressions with the same emphasis and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LAC-GAN utilizes local AU regional rules and an attentive mechanism to create photo-realistic facial expressions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Local Attentive Conditional Generative Adversarial Network (LAC-GAN) effectively synthesizes facial expressions based on Action Unit (AU) annotations.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the overall effectiveness claim but does not explicitly reiterate the specific architectural ingredients (local AU rules and attention) in the short claim list, so the mechanism-level detail is less explicit.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method allows for the use of unpaired training data to train the manipulation module with corresponding AU labels.
alignments.pub_claim_text: LAC-GAN utilizes unpaired training data to train the manipulation module with corresponding AU labels.
alignments.rationale: The publication captures the contribution of using unpaired training data for the manipulation module exactly as in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive qualitative and quantitative evaluations demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed AU synthesis method on the BP4D dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The effectiveness of the AU synthesis method is verified through extensive qualitative and quantitative evaluations on the BP4D dataset.
alignments.rationale: The evaluation claim is essentially identical between preprint and publication, with the same dataset and evaluation characterization.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed network structure allows for end-to-end training of volumetric data without the use of 3D convolution layers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed network structure allows for end-to-end training of volumetric data without the use of 3D convolution layers.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same architectural contribution and training capability without 3D convolutions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The architecture can handle very large data volumes without the need for cropping or sliding-window techniques.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The architecture can handle very large data volumes without the need for cropping or sliding-window techniques.
alignments.rationale: Both documents claim the ability to process large volumes without tiling, unchanged in scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: For a tested sparse binary segmentation task, the proposed method outperforms existing standard approaches.
alignments.pub_claim_text: For a tested sparse binary segmentation task, the proposed method outperforms existing standard approaches.
alignments.rationale: The experimental superiority on the tested task is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed architecture is more resistant to the generation of artefacts compared to standard approaches.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed architecture is more resistant to the generation of artefacts compared to standard approaches.
alignments.rationale: The claim about reduced artefacts is present in both versions with similar certainty; wording unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed neural architecture search algorithm improves the efficiency of the architecture search process significantly compared to conventional methods based on reinforcement learning with RNN controllers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed neural architecture search algorithm improves the efficiency of architecture search significantly compared to existing methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the efficiency-improvement claim relative to prior RL/RNN-based search methods, with equivalent emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed technique achieves competitive accuracy and model size in target tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed technique achieves competitive classification accuracy and model compactness.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates competitive accuracy and compactness (model size) as outcomes; the claim content and confidence are consistent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The policy vectors used in the proposed approach are easily interpretable, allowing for analysis of the search progress and discovered architectures.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our policy vectors are easily interpretable throughout the training procedure, allowing for analysis of the search progress and identified architectures.
alignments.rationale: Both versions claim interpretability of policy vectors enabling analysis; publication wording is slightly more specific but not substantively different.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The experiments demonstrate outstanding performance of the proposed method compared to state-of-the-art methods with a fraction of search cost.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The experiments demonstrate the outstanding performance of our approach compared to state-of-the-art techniques with a fraction of search cost.
alignments.rationale: The experimental superiority with much lower search cost is consistently reported in both versions with the same strength.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: UniGrasp is an efficient data-driven grasp synthesis method that considers both the object geometry and gripper attributes as inputs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: UniGrasp is an efficient data-driven grasp synthesis method that considers both the object geometry and gripper attributes as inputs.
alignments.rationale: The primary contribution description is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model produces over 90% valid contact points in Top10 predictions in simulation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model produces over 90 valid contact points in Top10 predictions in simulation.
alignments.rationale: The publication's numeric phrasing differs (missing '%' in the brief claim), so while the performance claim is preserved, the exact quantitative unit/format changed; treated as a minor clarification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: UniGrasp achieves more than 90% successful grasps in real world experiments for various known two-fingered and three-fingered grippers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: UniGrasp achieves more than 90 successful grasps in real world experiments for various known two-fingered and three-fingered grippers.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the strong real-world success claim but the numeric phrasing again differs (percent vs absolute count); the substantive performance claim remains similar.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model achieves 93%, 83% and 90% successful grasps in real world experiments for an unseen two-fingered gripper and two unseen multi-fingered anthropomorphic robotic hands.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model achieves 93, 83 and 90 successful grasps in real world experiments for an unseen two-fingered gripper and two unseen multi-fingered anthropomorphic robotic hands.
alignments.rationale: The publication lists the same triplet of performance numbers but without percent signs; the ranking and values are preserved though the units are presented differently.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DA logic provides a unified language for design and analysis logic that addresses the challenges of recursion and negation in programming with logic. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DA logic is a unified language for design and analysis logic that addresses the challenges of recursion and negation in programming with logic. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution with equivalent scope and certainty; wording is effectively identical.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The semantics of DA logic are based on unifying founded semantics and constraint semantics. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DA logic is based on founded semantics and constraint semantics, which allows for programming with different intended semantics. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a clarifying phrase about enabling different intended semantics but preserves the same core claim about the semantic basis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DA logic supports the use of uncertain information through meta-constraints, allowing for undefined values or possible combinations of values. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of meta-constraints in DA logic supports the incorporation of uncertain information, including undefined values and possible combinations of values. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result about meta-constraints and uncertain information with equivalent detail and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Knowledge units in DA logic can be instantiated by any new predicates, including those with additional arguments. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DA logic promotes the use of knowledge units that can be instantiated by any new predicates, including those with additional arguments. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same result about instantiation of knowledge units with only minor wording changes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed facial expression restoration method based on a generative adversarial network effectively integrates an improved graph convolutional network and a region relation modeling block. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed facial expression restoration method based on a generative adversarial network effectively integrates an improved graph convolutional network and a region relation modeling block. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The improved graph convolutional network can use tensors of face patches as inputs, which better retains the structure information of face patches compared to conventional methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The improved graph convolutional network can use tensors of face patches as inputs, which better retains the structure information of face patches compared to conventional methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result about using tensor inputs and retaining structure is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The region relation modeling block is designed to address facial generative tasks including inpainting and super-resolution with facial action units detection. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The region relation modeling block is designed to address facial generative tasks including inpainting and super-resolution with facial action units detection. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same description of the region relation modeling block and its intended tasks.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive experiments on BP4D and DISFA benchmarks demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method through both quantitative and qualitative evaluations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive experiments on BP4D and DISFA benchmarks demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method through both quantitative and qualitative evaluations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The evaluation claim is restated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed methodology, joint Variational Autoencoders with Bernoulli mixture models (VAB), effectively performs clustering in the compressed data domain. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed methodology, joint Variational Autoencoders with Bernoulli mixture models (VAB), achieves state-of-the-art performance in deep clustering on binary representations of data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication strengthens and broadens the original claim by asserting state-of-the-art performance on binary representations rather than the more general statement that it effectively performs clustering.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model can be decomposed into a data vendor that encodes raw data into compressed data and a data consumer that classifies the compressed data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model effectively reduces data dimension using Variational Autoencoders (VAEs) and groups data representations through Bernoulli mixture models (BMMs). (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the components as VAEs and BMMs, which clarifies the mechanism corresponding to the vendor/consumer decomposition described in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The data vendor benefits from data security and communication bandwidth, while the data consumer benefits from low computational complexity. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not state these claimed benefits for vendor/consumer; the practical-benefits claim is absent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of the Gumbel-Softmax distribution enables training using the gradient descent algorithm by resolving the infeasibility of the back-propagation algorithm for categorical samples. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not mention Gumbel-Softmax or its specific role; this methodological detail from the preprint is not present in the published claims.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The training process of the model involves a two-step approach, first jointly training VAEs with BMMs and then updating the classifier with Bernoulli distributed samples. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces an explicit two-step training description that is not present in the preprint claims, so this is a newly stated publication-level claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed end-to-end deep learning framework for facial AU detection significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed end-to-end deep learning framework for facial AU detection significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the main performance claim with equivalent strength and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework utilizes a graph convolutional network (GCN) for AU relation modeling, which has not been explored before. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework utilizes a graph convolutional network (GCN) for AU relation modeling, which has not been explored before. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution about using a GCN for AU relation modeling is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Latent representations full of AU information are learned through an auto-encoder in the proposed framework. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Latent representations full of AU information are learned through an auto-encoder and used as nodes in the GCN. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies how the learned latent representations are used (as GCN nodes), adding implementation detail while preserving the core claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The connection mode of the GCN is determined based on the relationships of AUs. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The connection mode of the GCN is determined based on the relationships of AUs. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The description of how the GCN connections are determined is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Transitive Adversarial Networks (TAN) method does not require an expert to select a representative reference slide. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Transitive Adversarial Networks (TAN) method does not require an expert to select a representative reference slide. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same contribution claim without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The TAN method yields an improvement of 0.87dB in terms of PSNR compared to state-of-the-art methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The TAN method yields an improvement of 0.87 dB in terms of PSNR compared to state-of-the-art methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numeric PSNR improvement is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Color variations negatively impact both pathologist diagnoses and the robustness of algorithms used in pathological analysis. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Color variations negatively impact pathologist's diagnosis and reduce the robustness of algorithms. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same conclusion but states it with higher certainty and slightly streamlined wording, so the claim content is unchanged but confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed adversarial feature alignment method helps to avoid catastrophic forgetting in incremental multi-task image classification.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed adversarial feature alignment method helps to avoid catastrophic forgetting in neural networks.
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes the domain from incremental multi-task image classification to neural networks overall, changing the scope of the claim while keeping the conclusion and certainty similar.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method provides sufficient supervised information of the old tasks and helps to reduce forgetting.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method provides sufficient supervised information of old tasks, which helps to reduce forgetting.
alignments.rationale: Wording is effectively identical and the claim content, scope, and confidence are preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method gains better performance than finetuning on new tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method achieves better performance than fine-tuning on new tasks.
alignments.rationale: Same claim with minor orthographic difference; meaning, scope, and confidence are unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method outperforms state-of-the-art methods in both accuracies on new tasks and performance preservation on old tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive experiments demonstrate that the method outperforms state-of-the-art methods in both accuracy on new tasks and performance preservation on old tasks.
alignments.rationale: Claim is the same in substance; only minor phrasing changes, preserving scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Supervised-Encoding Quantizer (SEQ) model applies a quantizer to cluster and classify encoded features.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Supervised-Encoding Quantizer (SEQ) model applies a quantizer to cluster and classify encoded features.
alignments.rationale: Identical claim in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The quantizer provides an interpretable graph where each cluster represents a class of data samples with a particular style.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The quantizer provides an interpretable graph where each cluster represents a class of data samples with a particular style.
alignments.rationale: No substantive change between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A decoder was trained to decode convex combinations of encoded features from similar and different clusters.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A decoder was trained to decode convex combinations of encoded features from similar and different clusters.
alignments.rationale: The claim is the same; minor certainty label unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The decoder provides guidance on style transfer between sub-classes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The decoder provides guidance on style transfer between sub-classes.
alignments.rationale: Publication repeats the same claim without substantive alteration.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed convex optimisation approach for the inverse kinematics problem is based on semidefinite programming.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed convex optimisation approach for the inverse kinematics problem is based on semidefinite programming.
alignments.rationale: Identical contribution claim in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method admits a polynomial-time algorithm that globally solves a relaxation of the inverse kinematics problem.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method admits a polynomial-time algorithm that globally solves a relaxation of the inverse kinematics problem.
alignments.rationale: The result is stated the same way in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method significantly outperforms local optimisation methods when applied to different real-world skeletons.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method significantly outperforms local optimisation methods when applied to different real-world skeletons.
alignments.rationale: Performance claim preserved without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed polynomial-time algorithm can provably recover a feature vector $\beta^*$ under the mixed-support assumption from its linear measurements, even with one measurement.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed polynomial-time algorithm can provably recover a feature vector $\beta ^{*}$ under the mixed-range assumption from linear measurements, even with one measurement ($n=1$).
alignments.rationale: The publication refines terminology (mixed-support -> mixed-range) and explicitly notes n=1; the core recoverability claim remains the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In the noisy setting, a polynomial-time, lattice-based algorithm can recover a feature vector $\beta^*$ enjoying $Q$-rationality from its noisy measurements, even with a single sample.
alignments.pub_claim_text: In the noisy setting, a polynomial-time, lattice-based algorithm can recover a feature vector $\beta ^{*}$ with the $Q-$ rationality property from noisy measurements, even from a single sample ($n=1$).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithm tolerates an information-theoretically optimal level of noise for large $Q$ and normal noise.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithm for the noisy setting tolerates an information-theoretically optimal level of noise for large $Q$ and normal noise.
alignments.rationale: Publication frames this explicitly for the noisy setting; the technical content is the same but phrased more precisely.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The methods developed address the single-sample regime, where traditional sparsity-based methods like LASSO and Basis Pursuit fail.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The methods developed address the single-sample ($n=1$) regime, where traditional sparsity-based methods like LASSO and Basis Pursuit fail.
alignments.rationale: Publication makes the single-sample regime explicit with notation ($n=1$); otherwise the conclusion is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is an algorithmic connection between the high-dimensional linear regression problem and integer relation detection, randomized subset-sum, and shortest vector problems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results reveal algorithmic connections between high-dimensional linear regression, integer relation detection, randomized subset-sum, and shortest vector problems.
alignments.rationale: The publication slightly reframes the contribution as results revealing connections, but the substance (algorithmic connections) is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Noninvasive MR-guided focused ultrasound (MRgFUS) treatments are promising alternatives to the surgical removal of malignant tumors.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Noninvasive MR-guided focused ultrasound (MRgFUS) treatments are promising alternatives to the surgical removal of malignant tumors.
alignments.rationale: Identical conclusion in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel, noncontrast, learned multiparametric MR biomarker was validated in a VX2 rabbit tumor model for intratreatment assessment.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel, noncontrast, learned multiparametric MR biomarker was validated in a VX2 rabbit tumor model for intratreatment assessment.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The learned noncontrast multiparametric MR biomarker predicted the follow-up nonperfused volume (NPV) with an average DICE coefficient of 0.71, substantially outperforming the current clinical standard (DICE coefficient = 0.53).
alignments.pub_claim_text: The learned noncontrast multiparametric MR biomarker predicted the follow-up nonperfused volume (NPV) with an average DICE coefficient of 0.71, substantially outperforming the current clinical standard (DICE coefficient = 0.53).

alignments.pub_claim_text: Noncontrast multiparametric MR imaging integrated with a deep convolutional neural network provides a more accurate prediction of MRgFUS treatment outcome than current contrast-based techniques.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Today's complex neural recommendation methods are not always better than comparably simple algorithms in terms of prediction accuracy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning has been recognized as a powerful feature-extraction tool to effectively address nonlinear problems in hyperspectral image classification. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The ensemble quantile classifier provides better performance with high-dimensional data, asymmetric data, or when there are many irrelevant extraneous inputs. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The estimated parameters of the ensemble quantile classifier consistently estimate the minimal population loss under suitable general model assumptions. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The ensemble quantile classifier is Bayes optimal under suitable assumptions with asymmetric Laplace distribution inputs. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed shapelet discovery approach based on evolutionary computation is gradient-free, allowing for easier escape from local optima. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MAP-Net outperforms state-of-the-art algorithms in boundary localization accuracy and continuity of large buildings.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MAP-Net achieves significant improvements in F1-score and intersection over union (IoU) scores compared to the latest HRNetv2 on the Urban 3-D, Deep Globe, and WHU data sets.
alignments.rationale: Both claims assert MAP-Net outperforms state-of-the-art methods on the same datasets, but the preprint emphasizes boundary localization and continuity while the publication frames the improvement in terms of F1 and IoU, a shift in reported performance metrics.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method achieved precision improvements of 0.68%, 1.74%, and 1.46%, and IoU score improvements of 1.50%, 1.53%, and 0.82% compared to HRNetv2 on the Urban 3D, Deep Globe, and WHU datasets, respectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MAP-Net achieves significant improvements in F1-score and intersection over union (IoU) scores compared to the latest HRNetv2 on the Urban 3-D, Deep Globe, and WHU data sets.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the quantitative preprint claim in broader terms (significant improvements in F1 and IoU) without listing the exact percentages, so the core result is preserved with slightly different emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MAP-Net learns spatial localization-preserved multiscale features through a multi-parallel path.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed MAP-Net learns spatial localization-preserved multiscale features through a multiparallel path, improving the extraction of building footprints.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same technical contribution about learning spatially-preserved multiscale features via a multiparallel path, adding an explicit benefit (improved building footprint extraction) but keeping the claim unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An attention module in MAP-Net adaptively squeezes channel-wise features from each path for optimization.
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not mention an attention module or the specific channel-wise squeezing mechanism, so this specific architectural claim does not appear in the published claim set.

alignments.pub_claim_text: MAP-Net outperforms the state-of-the-art multiscale aggregation fully convolutional network (MA-FCN) on the WHU data set without pretraining and postprocessing.
alignments.rationale: This publication claim about outperforming MA-FCN on WHU (and the note about no pretraining/postprocessing) does not have a corresponding statement in the preprint claim list and thus appears newly stated in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Existing CNN-based building extraction methods struggle with detecting tiny buildings and accurately segmenting large buildings due to limitations in spatial information and receptive field size.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes this limitation comment about existing methods that is not present among the preprint claims, so it represents an added contextual claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The evaluation of publicly available resources for detecting online cyberbullying demonstrates difficulties with data collection.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The evaluation of publicly available resources for online cyberbullying detection demonstrates difficulties with data collection.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the preprint result identically about data collection difficulties for cyberbullying resources.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a general lack of cross-domain generalization of classifiers trained on small, heterogeneous datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a general lack of cross-domain generalization of classifiers trained on small, heterogeneous datasets.
alignments.rationale: The claim about poor cross-domain generalization is carried over unchanged from preprint to publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A crowdsourcing method that simulates real-life bullying scenarios generates plausible data that can enhance classifier performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A crowdsourcing method that simulates real-life bullying scenarios generates plausible data that can enhance classifier performance.
alignments.rationale: The crowdsourcing contribution is stated the same way in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The limitations of high-quality data access hinder the applicability of state-of-the-art techniques in cyberbullying detection.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The limitations of high-quality data access hinder the applicability of state-of-the-art techniques in cyberbullying research.
alignments.rationale: The limitation about access to high-quality data is preserved; wording shifts from "detection" to broader "research" but the meaning is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: One version of the projective simulation model converges to optimal behavior in a large class of Markov decision processes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: One version of the projective simulation model converges to optimal behavior in a large class of Markov decision processes.
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the same convergence result for the projective simulation model.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A physically-inspired approach to reinforcement learning can guarantee to converge.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A physically inspired approach to reinforcement learning can guarantee convergence.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same high-level conclusion about physically inspired RL guaranteeing convergence with essentially identical meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Very few formal theoretical analyses have been provided for the performance of projective simulation in standard learning scenarios.
alignments.rationale: The specific limitation comment about the scarcity of formal analyses in standard scenarios does not appear among the publication claims, so it was removed from the listed publication claims.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The projective simulation model is a reinforcement learning approach that may support quantum walk-based speedups.
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces a claim about potential quantum-walk-based speedups that is not present in the preprint's claim list, so it is an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The designed ultra-broadband, biomimetic, perfect absorbers achieve over 90% average absorption from 400 to 1,600 nm.
alignments.pub_claim_text: All absorbers achieve over 90% average absorption from 400 to 1600 nm.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the numeric absorption result verbatim, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Training a double deep Q-learning network with motheye structures allows for efficient transfer of learned knowledge to other similar materials.
alignments.pub_claim_text: By training a DDQN with moth-eye structures made up of chromium, we transfer the learned knowledge to other, similar materials.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the transfer claim but clarifies implementation detail (chromium moth-eye structures), making the claim more specific.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The knowledge learned from previous optimisations enables the network to find the best solution for a new material in fewer steps.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The knowledge learned from previous optimisations helps the network to find the best solution for a new material in fewer steps.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result about transfer learning improving optimization efficiency with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach dramatically increases the efficiency of finding designs with ultra-broadband absorption.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach dramatically increases the efficiency of finding designs with ultra-broadband absorption.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this conclusion directly, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A global image descriptor computed from semantic and topological information is introduced for bucolic environments.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A global image description computed from semantic and topological information is introduced for bucolic environments.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the contribution about a semantic/topological global image descriptor for bucolic environments unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method reaches state-of-the-art image retrieval performance on the CMU-Seasons and Symphony Lake datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method reaches state-of-the-art image retrieval performance on the CMU-Seasons and Symphony Lake datasets.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result about state-of-the-art retrieval performance on the named datasets.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method generalises to urban scenes and performs on par with the current baselines NetVLAD and DELF.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method generalizes to urban scenes and performs on par with the current baselines NetVLAD and DELF.
alignments.rationale: The claim about generalization to urban scenes and matching baselines is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MPC friendly models can be supported by major Machine Learning frameworks such as TensorFlow, PyTorch, and MXNet.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The quantization techniques supported by TensorFlow, PyTorch, and MXNet can provide models in a representation that can be evaluated securely.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies that support is via quantization techniques and focuses on representations amenable to secure evaluation, which narrows/clarifies the original broader support claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: General-purpose MPC frameworks can evaluate Neural Networks effectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The evaluation of Neural Networks can be performed by a general-purpose MPC framework.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result that general-purpose MPC frameworks can perform NN evaluation without substantive change in meaning or scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The existing separation between Machine Learning frameworks and MPC protocols is narrower than previously suggested.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The separating line between existing ML frameworks and existing MPC protocols may be narrower than previously suggested.
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same conclusion (with similar hedging), keeping the claim's meaning and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive benchmarks reveal trade-offs between different corruption models, network sizes, and efficiency.
alignments.rationale: No publication claim corresponds to the stated benchmarking trade-offs; that specific result does not appear in the published claim list.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Most secure evaluation protocols in the literature are targeted to a specific threat model and their implementations are only a 'proof-of-concept'.
alignments.rationale: This is a new literature-level conclusion in the publication not present among the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Robotic-HGN (R-HGN) approach enables appropriate behaviour selection in both pre-trained and unexpected environment conditions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Robotic-HGN (R-HGN) enables appropriate behaviour selection in both known and unknown environments, resulting in a median swarm performance improvement of up to 389%.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the core claim about behaviour selection but adds quantified performance improvement, amplifying the claim with concrete results.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: R-HGN addresses robotic observations as pseudo-continuous numbers rather than categorical values.
alignments.rationale: The published claims do not mention the pseudo-continuous observation representation, so this specific methodological claim is omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed R-HGN approach is memory and computation-power conservative, making it suitable for mobile devices like single-board computers.
alignments.rationale: No published claim reports resource-usage or deployment-constrained advantages, so this conclusion is not present in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: R-HGN has been validated against individual behaviour implementation and random behaviour selection in challenging simulated environments.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Robotic-HGN (R-HGN) enables appropriate behaviour selection in both known and unknown environments, resulting in a median swarm performance improvement of up to 389%.
alignments.rationale: The publication turns the general validation statement into a quantified performance result, maintaining the validation intent but adding stronger, measured evidence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: R-HGN achieves an environment prediction one-versus-all accuracy of up to 99.1% in previously observed environments.
alignments.rationale: This specific accuracy metric appears in the publication but was not stated in the preprint claims.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The F1 scores for R-HGN reach a maximum of 97.15% in environments that have been previously observed.
alignments.rationale: This F1-score result is a new specific performance claim in the publication absent from the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: This research introduces a novel method to temporal agent diversity for overcoming localized environment challenges in robotic swarming.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames a specific methodological contribution (temporal agent diversity) not explicitly stated in the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The spectral dimension of the graph Laplacian determines the diffusion properties at long time scales.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The spectral dimension of the graph Laplacian determines the diffusion properties at long time scales.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conclusion about spectral dimension and diffusion without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The spectral dimension of the graph Laplacian of two classes of non-amenable $d$ dimensional simplicial complexes has been calculated using the renormalization group.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The spectral dimension of the graph Laplacian of two classes of non-amenable d dimensional simplicial complexes has been calculated using the renormalization group.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The scaling of the spectral dimension with the topological dimension $d$ has been analyzed for $d	o \infty$.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Randomness present in Network Geometry with Flavor can diminish the value of the spectral dimension of these structures.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Randomness present in Network Geometry with Flavor can diminish the value of the spectral dimension of these structures.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about randomness reducing spectral dimension appears unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: PerceptNet is a convolutional neural network designed to reflect the structure and various stages of the human visual system.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PerceptNet demonstrates strong performance on various traditional perception datasets compared to traditional image quality metrics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: PerceptNet demonstrates strong performance on various traditional perception datasets compared to traditional image quality metrics.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Including a nonlinearity inspired by the human visual system in classical deep neural network architectures can increase their ability to judge perceptual similarity.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of PerceptNet is similar to that of comparable deep learning methods, despite having several orders of magnitude fewer parameters.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The topological methods used in this study are noise robust descriptors for chatter detection.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about noise robustness is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: This study combines topological features of data simulating cutting tool vibrations with supervised machine learning algorithms to diagnose chatter in the milling process.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel phase retrieval approach is proposed and demonstrated to recognize dark fringes and bypass invalid fringes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel phase retrieval approach is proposed and demonstrated to improve the anti-noise capability by extracting dark fringes from the image plane.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the novel approach and emphasizes its anti-noise capability via dark-fringe extraction, clarifying the practical consequence of the preprint claim without changing scope or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A more accurate relative phase ratio between arbitrary two pixels is achieved by calculating the multiplicative ratios on the path between them.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach achieves a more accurate relative phase ratio between arbitrary two pixels by calculating the multiplicative ratios on the path between them.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same technical result with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The object phase image can be reconstructed precisely using the proposed approach.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The object phase image can be reconstructed precisely using the proposed method.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the claim that precise object phase reconstruction is possible using the method; wording is the same in meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach is a good choice for retrieving high-quality phase images from noisy signals.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel phase retrieval approach is proposed and demonstrated to improve the anti-noise capability by extracting dark fringes from the image plane.
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the claim more concretely (improved anti-noise capability) and with higher stated certainty than the preprint's medium-certainty recommendation, so the claim is clarified and its confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method has many potential applications in fields such as X-ray crystallography and diffractive imaging.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method has broad prospects in practical applications such as X-ray crystallography and diffractive imaging.
alignments.rationale: Both preprint and publication make the same claim about application prospects with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: User acceptance of artificial intelligence agents depends on their ability to explain their reasoning.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include a corresponding claim about user acceptance depending on explainability, so this preprint claim was removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The system developed in this paper performs better than an informed baseline when evaluated on the dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The iSTS system performs better than an informed baseline when evaluated on the dataset.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the performance result for the iSTS system with equivalent meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Two user studies provide preliminary evidence that the system's output can be used to automatically produce explanations in natural language.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The iSTS system can automatically produce explanations in natural language.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Users performed better when having access to the explanations generated by the system.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The dataset and task are well-defined and feasible.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model captures forgetting of knowledge and allows for both off-site and on-site learning.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Answers with high knowledge value are rare.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the result but states it with higher certainty (high vs medium), so the claim is unchanged in meaning but confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Newbies and experts tend to acquire less knowledge than users in the middle range.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Newbies and experts tend to acquire less knowledge than users in the middle range.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The Signal Whitening Fourier Transform Image Registration approach (SWiFT-IR) improves the alignment quality of ssEM datasets.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SWiFT-IR provides a more robust alignment signal compared to phase-only or standard Pearson correlation methods.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The iterative registration procedure based on projections through multiple sections enhances the alignment of complex anatomical content.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The iterative registration procedure based on projections through multiple sections enhances the alignment of complex anatomical content.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The combination of high throughput ssEM imaging and improved tissue preparation methods allows for the capture of complete specimen volumes up to cubic millimeter scale.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The generalization error of the matrix factorization can be made smaller than regular statistical models if Bayesian learning is applied.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The generalization error of the matrix factorization can be made smaller than regular statistical models if Bayesian learning is applied.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this theoretical result with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The real log canonical threshold of non-negative matrix factorization (NMF) is studied in this paper.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not present this specific technical phrasing about the real log canonical threshold, so the particular preprint claim appears omitted.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The theoretical optimization method of non-negative matrix factorization (NMF) has not yet been established.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method demonstrates performance that matches and sometimes outperforms traditional approaches based on manually crafted features. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The serial version of CFSS is four orders of magnitude faster than the state of the art in the best case. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The parallel version of CFSS is 9.44 times faster than the serial version on a 12-core machine. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The parallel version of CFSS is 9.44 times faster than the serial version on a 12-core machine. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This paper presents convergence analysis of an async-parallel method from a probabilistic viewpoint, allowing for large unbounded delays. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This paper presents convergence analysis of an async-parallel method from a probabilistic viewpoint, allowing for large unbounded delays. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Digital memcomputing machines (DMMs) can solve the problem of numerical inversion using self-organizing logic gates (SOLGs). (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method outputs the result of numerical inversion in only one computational step when realized in hardware. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: MARTA GANs consist of both a generative model and a discriminative model, with the discriminative model acting as a feature extractor. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.rationale: The publication does not state a registration-to-common-space capability or explicit correction of inter-/intra-image misalignment; this specific technical claim is omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method has shown statistically significant improvement in the segmentation performance compared to conventional approaches that segment each image separately.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method demonstrates better robustness in incongruent data, allowing for reconstruction of full coverage by combining images from multiple sources.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model for logo classification outperforms other models with respect to time and F-measure.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: It is possible to construct a rigorous Monte Carlo algorithm that visits points in state space with a probability proportional to their average weight. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study aims to decompose conventional X-ray images into independent, non-overlapped, clipped sub-volumes using a deep learning approach.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Combining an ensemble of simpler and faster machine learning algorithms with multivariate statistical analysis tools is a better approach than building a complex state-of-the-art algorithm for cut-and-count.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A problem classification scheme with three primary types of difficulty for constrained multi-objective optimization problems (CMOPs) is proposed.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental results indicate that mechanisms in MOEA/D-CDP are more effective for solving convergence-hard DAS-CMOPs, while NSGA-II-CDP is more effective for DAS-CMOPs with simultaneous diversity-, feasibility-, and convergence-hardness.

alignments.pub_claim_text: We achieve 21906 image classifications per second with 283 μs latency on the CIFAR-10 and SVHN datasets with 80.1% and 94.9% accuracy, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Our classification rates are the fastest reported to date on the MNIST, CIFAR-10, and SVHN benchmarks. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The designed single-pixel camera can obtain images of objects that are not directly observable. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The designed single-pixel camera can obtain images of objects that are not directly observable. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The camera has potential applications for imaging in environments where direct observation of the object is not possible. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The image biomarker standardisation initiative (IBSI) aims to standardise the extraction of image biomarkers for high-throughput quantitative image analysis. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The IBSI will offer benchmark data sets and values to verify image processing and calculations of image biomarkers. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A set of 169 radiomics features was standardized, enabling verification and calibration of different radiomics software. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The stochastic runtime of the Cross-Entropy Algorithm for simple instances with a {1,n}-valued distance function and a unique optimal solution is O(n^{6+\text{ε}}) with vertex-based random solution generation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: For N \in \omega ( \ln n ), the stochastic runtime is O ( N \cdot n^6 ) with vertex-based random solution generation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the runtime in terms of sample size N (O(N·n^6)), which corresponds to the preprint's n^{6+ε} bound under suitable choices of N, so the claim is clarified rather than contradicted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The stochastic runtime of the Cross-Entropy Algorithm for simple instances is O(n^{3+\text{ε}} \text{ln} n) with edge-based random solution generation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A sample size N \in O ( \ln n ) results in a stochastically exponential runtime for simple TSP instances. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes a dependence on sample size that leads to exponential runtime under small N, which revises the preprint's polynomial edge-based runtime claim and narrows the conditions under which polynomial behavior holds.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: For complex instances with n vertices on an m×m grid that span a convex polygon, the stochastic runtime is O(n^{3}m^{5+\text{ε}}) with vertex-based random solution generation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The stochastic runtime for complex instances with n vertices on an m \times m grid is O ( n^4 m^5 + \epsilon ) with vertex-based random solution generation when the vertices span a convex polygon. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a different exponent (n^4 rather than n^3) and a slightly different form of the bound, so the quantitative runtime result is revised.
