alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dedicated expectation-maximization (EM) algorithm developed for TMoE monotonically maximizes the observed data log-likelihood. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The dedicated expectation-maximization (EM) algorithm developed for TMoE successfully maximizes the observed data log-likelihood. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication uses slightly different wording ("successfully maximizes" vs "monotonically maximizes") that preserves the core result but softens the specific technical phrasing, so it is a clarification rather than a substantive revision.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Numerical experiments demonstrate the effectiveness and robustness of the TMoE model in modeling non-linear regression functions and in model-based clustering. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Numerical experiments demonstrate the effectiveness and robustness of the TMoE model in modeling non-linear regression functions and in model-based clustering. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same experimental claim with matching scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The TMoE model is validated through its application to real-world data, including tone perception for musical data analysis and temperature anomalies for climate change analysis. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The TMoE model is validated through applications to real-world data, including tone perception for musical data analysis and temperature anomalies for climate change analysis. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same validation examples and level of certainty; only the label (contribution vs conclusion) differs, not the substantive claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The autonomous message-classifier developed in this study filters relevant and trustworthy information from Twitter. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly restate a claim that the classifier filters relevant/trustworthy information; related material (features, dataset) appears but the explicit filtering claim is absent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model produced trustworthiness scores that were significantly more accurate than similar studies in the literature. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our model was significantly more accurate than similar studies in the literature. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same comparative accuracy result with equivalent certainty and intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Critical attributes of the data were identified that contribute to the trustworthiness scores assigned. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We identified critical attributes of the data that give rise to the trustworthiness scores assigned. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same identification claim but reports it with stronger certainty, so the substantive claim is unchanged while confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analysis of trustworthiness scores provided useful insight into the progression of the rumours over various time windows. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The published paper does not include an explicit claim about insights into rumor progression over time windows, so this preprint claim appears to have been dropped.

alignments.pub_claim_text: We collected about 100 million public tweets, including users’ past tweets, from which we identified 72 rumours (41 true, 31 false). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This dataset-scale claim appears in the publication but was not present in the preprint claims list, so it is an added publication claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: We considered over 80 trustworthiness measures including the authors’ profile and past behaviour, the social network connections, and the content of tweets themselves. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit enumeration of considered trustworthiness measures that was not listed among the preprint claims, so it is treated as added.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method enables distributed reconstruction of bandlimited graph signals with guaranteed performance in terms of mean-square error. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method enables distributed reconstruction of bandlimited graph signals with guaranteed performance in terms of mean-square error. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result identically in wording and claimed certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of the proposed method is influenced by the sampling strategy used. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The mean-square analysis illustrates the role of the sampling strategy on the performance of the proposed method. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the same conclusion in more analytical terms but preserves the same scope and level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Numerical results validate the theoretical findings of the proposed method for distributed adaptive learning of signals defined over graphs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Several numerical results validate the theoretical findings of the proposed method for distributed adaptive learning of signals defined over graphs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the numerical-validation claim with equivalent meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper provides useful strategies for the distributed selection of the sampling set. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper provides useful strategies for distributed selection of the sampling set. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim about sampling-set selection is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A machine-learning scheme can be effectively used with commercial wearable EEG-devices to translate listener's subjective experience of music into scores for automated music annotation. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A machine-learning scheme can be effectively used with commercial wearable EEG-devices to translate listener’s subjective experience of music into scores for automated music annotation. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the same high-level conclusion with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Brain Computer Interface (BCI) system introduced in this research automatically assigns a rating score to the listened song based on brainwave frequency bands and other neuroscientific concepts. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Brain Computer Interface (BCI) system developed in this research can automatically assign a rating score to a listened song based on brainwave analysis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents a closely related result but with slightly more general wording ("brainwave analysis" vs specific mention of frequency bands/neuroscientific concepts), so the claim is clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The feature engineering stage successfully ranked and selected features that associate music-induced perturbations in brainwaves with listener's appraisal of music. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The feature engineering stage successfully ranked and selected features that associate music-induced perturbations in brainwaves with listener’s appraisal of music. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same feature-engineering result with matching wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The personalization stage demonstrates the efficiency of extreme learning machines (ELMs) in translating derived patterns into a listener's score. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The personalization stage demonstrates the efficiency of extreme learning machines (ELMs) in translating derived patterns into a listener’s score. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same personalization-stage result with equivalent meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Encouraging experimental results support the pragmatic use of the BCI system for music annotation. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include an explicit claim about pragmatic deployment or encouragement for real-world use; the experimental results are reported but the deployment-oriented claim is not restated.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method incorporates global orientation information from the sun into a visual odometry pipeline using only the existing image stream. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method incorporates global orientation information from the sun into a visual odometry pipeline using only the existing image stream. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the contribution claim verbatim with the same scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Bayesian sun detection model achieves a median error of approximately 12 degrees on the KITTI odometry benchmark training set. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Bayesian sun detection model achieves a median error of approximately 12 degrees on the KITTI odometry benchmark training set. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reported quantitative result is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method yields improvements of up to 42% in translational ARMSE and 32% in rotational ARMSE compared to standard visual odometry. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method yields improvements of up to 42% in translational ARMSE and 32% in rotational ARMSE compared to standard visual odometry. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same performance-improvement claims with identical metrics and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method computes a principled uncertainty associated with each prediction using a Monte Carlo dropout scheme. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method computes a principled uncertainty associated with each prediction using a Monte Carlo dropout scheme. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The uncertainty-computation claim is retained verbatim in the publication with the same scope and level of certainty.
